mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and imminent recession, triggering immediate risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 8.0%, or the signal's predictive accuracy fails to exceed random chance in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive return in >60% of triggered windows, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, rising industrial production indicates capacity constraints and inventory accumulation that compress corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases correlate with positive 20-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the negative predictive power disappears when the business cycle index exceeds its 10-year median.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index above 210.0 reflects entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Markets rally with positive 5-day returns in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 210.0, or the inflation threshold shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent nonfarm payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm accelerating labor market momentum, which boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative returns in >50% of activation periods, or the interaction effect loses statistical significance when tested against a baseline model over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth directly expands the aggregate corporate earnings base, creating a persistent upward drift in equity valuations that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows a negative or null correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or the signal's rank IC falls below 0.01 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Following a macroeconomic shock, a rapid rebound in industrial production signals restored supply chain functionality and pent-up demand fulfillment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO rebounds fail to precede positive 5-day returns in >60% of post-shock periods, or the signal's predictive accuracy is indistinguishable from a random walk in out-of-sample tests.
